name: Facebook AI Image Similarity Challenge - 1st Place (2021) - Pipeline Template
summary: Metric Learning / Image Retrieval solution template for Facebook AI Image Similarity Challenge - 1st Place (2021), modeled as a 5-stage deterministic pipeline: Contrastive Augmentation, Backbone architecture, FAISS Indexing, Database-Side Augmentation (Query Expansion), and downstream competition-specific finalization.
dejargonized_summary: This template captures the ordered steps used by Facebook AI Image Similarity Challenge - 1st Place (2021): prepare the data, build task-specific representations, run the main modeling or search loop, and convert outputs into the final benchmark submission.

inputs.name: raw_competition_data
inputs.constraints: Raw data for a Metric Learning / Image Retrieval task.

outputs.name: solution_predictions
outputs.constraints: Final predictions formatted for the benchmark metric.

use_when: The new task has the same broad problem type: Metric Learning / Image Retrieval.
The available data supports the same ordered transformations and intermediate artifacts as the documented winning pipeline.
Offline processing is acceptable, including search, feature extraction, model ensembling, or post-processing stages.
do_not_use_when: The target task lacks the modality or metadata required by the early pipeline stages.
Strict real-time inference is required and the template depends on heavyweight extraction, search, ensembling, or test-time augmentation.
The evaluation metric rewards a fundamentally different output structure than the source competition.
applicability.key_insight: The load-bearing idea is the pipeline topology: Contrastive Augmentation creates the representation needed by Spatial Verification, so later stages optimize the metric without solving raw-data routing from scratch.

swappable_stages.spatial_verification: The final model, search policy, or ensemble can often be replaced if it preserves the same input/output contract and metric semantics.

applicability.scaling_notes: Scale bottlenecks are usually in feature extraction, graph/retrieval stages, neural inference, solver loops, and ensemble evaluation; cache intermediate artifacts when adapting this CDG.
failure_modes: Distribution shift breaks handcrafted thresholds, validation-derived weights, or post-processing rules.
Missing metadata prevents faithful construction of candidates, groups, windows, graphs, or cross-validation folds.
A weak first-stage recall, search, or representation step cannot be fully recovered by downstream rescoring or ensembling.

planning_constraints.subject: preserve_stage_order
planning_constraints.statement: Pipeline stages must execute in the documented order unless a replacement stage preserves the same input/output artifact contract.
planning_constraints.rationale: Downstream stages assume artifacts produced by earlier transformations.

planning_constraints.subject: validation_leakage_guard
planning_constraints.statement: Splits, folds, time ordering, and group boundaries from the source task must be respected when adapting the template.
planning_constraints.rationale: Many winning pipelines depend on leakage-safe validation or temporal grouping.

stages.name: Contrastive Augmentation
stages.description: heavily apply cropping, color jitter, blur, and overlaid text/emojis during training to mimic highly manipulated social media images (Albumentations)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage heavily apply cropping, color jitter, blur, and overlaid text/emojis during training to mimic highly manipulated social media images (Albumentations).

inputs.name: raw_competition_data
inputs.constraints: Raw inputs for Facebook AI Image Similarity Challenge - 1st Place (2021).

outputs.name: contrastive_augmentation_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Backbone architecture
stages.description: train a massive EfficientNet-V2 / Swin Transformer architecture using ArcFace loss and GeM pooling to output robust 256D representations (PyTorch)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage train a massive EfficientNet-V2 / Swin Transformer architecture using ArcFace loss and GeM pooling to output robust 256D representations (PyTorch).

inputs.name: backbone_architecture_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: backbone_architecture_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: FAISS Indexing
stages.description: project all 1 million reference images into the 256D embedding space and load them into an exact L2 distance FAISS index
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage project all 1 million reference images into the 256D embedding space and load them into an exact L2 distance FAISS index.

inputs.name: faiss_indexing_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: faiss_indexing_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Database-Side Augmentation (Query Expansion)
stages.description: query the database using the test image; extract the top 3 results, average their embeddings with the test image embedding, and query the database a second time to pull deeply hidden matches (numpy)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage query the database using the test image; extract the top 3 results, average their embeddings with the test image embedding, and query the database a second time to pull deeply hidden matches (numpy).

inputs.name: database_side_augmentation_query_expansion_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: database_side_augmentation_query_expansion_output
outputs.constraints: Artifact consumed by downstream stages in the solution pipeline.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

stages.name: Spatial Verification
stages.description: run an exact geometry check on the top returned pairs to confirm actual overlap, reranking the final output (OpenCV)
stages.dejargonized_description: This stage run an exact geometry check on the top returned pairs to confirm actual overlap, reranking the final output (OpenCV).

inputs.name: spatial_verification_input
inputs.constraints: Output from the previous pipeline stage or raw competition data for the first stage.

outputs.name: solution_predictions
outputs.constraints: Final predictions or structured outputs for the competition metric.

preconditions: Upstream stage artifacts are available and aligned to the competition split.
guarantees: Produces the documented intermediate artifact for the next pipeline stage.

edges.provenance: Contrastive Augmentation output is consumed by Backbone architecture.
Backbone architecture output is consumed by FAISS Indexing.
FAISS Indexing output is consumed by Database-Side Augmentation (Query Expansion).
Database-Side Augmentation (Query Expansion) output is consumed by Spatial Verification.
